Class MyApp

Application
com.example.codebase.MyApp
public class MyApp extends Application
The application-level class for the Timely app, serving as the entry point for global initialization logic.

Declared in AndroidManifest.xml via the android:name attribute on the <application> tag. Android instantiates this class before any

invalid reference 
android.app.Activity
,
invalid reference 
android.app.Service
, or
invalid reference 
android.content.BroadcastReceiver
in the app process.

Currently responsible for eagerly initializing the AppDatabase singleton so that the first database access on the main thread does not incur cold-start latency.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    MyApp()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    onCreate()
    Called when the application process is starting, before any app components (excluding content providers) have been created.

  • Constructor Details

    • MyApp

      public MyApp()

  • Method Details

    • onCreate

      public void onCreate()
      Called when the application process is starting, before any app components (excluding content providers) have been created.

      Warms up the AppDatabase singleton by calling AppDatabase.getInstance() eagerly. Without this call, the first component to access the database would pay the initialization cost synchronously, which could cause jank if it occurs on the main thread.
